core_mechanism: reduction of a system with infinitely many coupled degrees of freedom to a single self-consistent nonlinear integral (fixed-point) equation whose solution yields the aggregate observable by quadrature, solved by iteration in one parameter regime and by closed-form asymptotics via a Riemann-Hilbert analytic-factorization structure in the opposite regime
brief_reason: The abstract states that in all cases the free energy follows by quadratures from the solution of a single nonlinear integral equation solved by iteration, with higher corrections obtained from its Riemann-Hilbert form, which is exactly a self-consistent fixed-point integral equation with an analytic-factorization asymptotic scheme.
